The '''assault battle droid''', also known as '''B1 assault droid''' or '''assault droid''' was a variation of the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] and the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. They often used heavy weapons and were designed for demolition of large fortified objectives.<ref name="BFII" /> Improved models were known as '''B1 Elite Assault''' droids.

==Characteristics==
[[File:B1_assault_droid.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A B1 assault droid.]]
Assault battle droids were [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] variants,<ref name="CWA" /> stood 1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, with [[Color/Legends|black]] sensors and tan plating constructed by [[Baktoid Armor Workshop/Legends|Baktoid Armor Workshop]].<ref name="Databank" /> They had [[Gender/Legends|masculine]] [[Programmer/Legends|programming]]<ref name="BFII" /> The heavy assault [[Droid/Legends|droids]] stood out from the standard [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], having a blue or dark red torso, occasionally with other red markings, similar to [[OOM pilot battle droid/Legends|OOM pilot battle droid]] and [[OOM-series security droid/Legends|OOM security battle droids]], respectively, though they were a different type of droid.<ref name="BF" /><ref name="BFII" />

The standard armament of B1 assault droids was a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]].<ref name="CWA" /> However, assault droids were often produced as anti-armored units used to destroy Republic turrets and vehicles on the battlefield using their primary weapon, the [[E-60R missile launcher]]. Supplementing the launcher was an [[SE-14 blaster pistol]] for taking out close-range, individual targets, and 3 [[V-1 thermal detonator]]s. They also deployed [[HX2 antipersonnel mine]]s mines to guard important positions.

==History==
[[File:AssaultBattleDroidE60RMissileLauncher-Databank.jpg|thumb|right|200px|An assault battle droid.]]
Assault battle droids were deployed during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] and the [[Kashyyyk Trade Rebellion]] during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]].<ref name="BF" />

They were later seen on many battles of the Clone Wars, such as the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], the [[First Battle of Kamino|Assault on Kamino]], the [[Battle of Rhen Var (Clone Wars)|Battle of Rhen Var]],<ref name="BF" /> the [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of Muunilinst]],<ref name="CW2">{{CW|2}}</ref> the [[Defense of Maridun/Legends|Defense of Maridun]], the [[First Battle of Felucia/Legends|First Battle of Felucia]], the [[Battle of Christophsis/Legends|Battle of Christophsis]], the [[Battle of Kamino/Legends|Battle of Kamino]], the [[Skirmish on Carlac]], the [[Battle of Umbara/Legends|Battle of Umbara]],<ref name="CWA" /> the [[Battle of Mygeeto]], the [[Battle of Felucia (Outer Rim Sieges)/Legends|Battle of Felucia]], the [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]] and the [[Battle of Utapau/Legends|Battle of Utapau]].<ref name="BFII" />

Following the end of the Clone Wars, they saw [[Battle of Mustafar (Imperial Period)|action]] on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] with [[Gizor Dellso]]'s [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]].<ref name="BFII" />

==Behind the scenes==
Assault droids first appeared in [[Chapter 2 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|''Clone Wars Chapter 2'']],<ref name="CW2" /> and served as the Separatist heavy-weapons unit in the ''[[Battlefront]]'' series.<ref name="BFII" />

In ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'' the assault battle droids had blue markings resembling those of the [[OOM pilot battle droid/Legends|pilot battle droids]],<ref name="BF" /> but in ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' they had red markings that resemble the [[OOM-series security droid/Legends|security battle droids]].<ref name="BFII" /> Furthermore, in some cutscenes of ''Battlefront II''<nowiki/>'s campaign, some assault droids can be seen with the blue markings of the previous game.<ref name="BFII" />
